methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Nascent Materials is a seed-stage climate and energy company that has raised $2.3M to date. The valuation analysis draws on two methodologies anchored to a comparable set of nine private frontier-tech companies and one public peer (LNZA), with three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private comparables used directly in the peer-median calculation.

The stage-progression method applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement, using the $2.3M raised as a valuation proxy, yielding $6M. The peer-median method derives from three climate and energy companies at Series A stage: Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M), producing a median of $150M with a range of $110M–$200M. These methods reflect substantially different risk-adjusted valuations, reflecting the gap between early-stage capital velocity and later-stage market confirmation.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $5M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $78M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty inherent in seed-stage valuation: the stage-progression result anchors to capital deployed rather than market validation, while the peer-median extrapolates from companies further along development and revenue trajectories. Key uncertainties include technical risk, path to commercialization, and whether peer comparables accurately reflect Nascent's specific technical and market position within the frontier-energy landscape.
